title: Shohei Ohtani reaches 350 career home runs between Japan and the United States
summary: The Dodgers outfielder hits his 22nd home run of the season, a milestone he achieves before the All-Star Game.

title: Rintaro Sasaki selected by the Miami Marlins in the eighth round of the MLB draft
summary: The Japanese hitter, projected as a first-round pick in Japan, was chosen in a later spot in the Major League draft.

title: Republican Senator Lindsey Graham dies at 71 from sudden illness
summary: The politician, an ally of Trump and staunch supporter of Ukraine, was remembered by the former president as a true patriot.

title: United States bombs 140 military targets in Iran in a new wave of attacks
summary: The US Central Command reported that the attacks struck Iranian missile facilities and ammunition warehouses.

title: Oil soars on fears of closure of the Strait of Hormuz after threats from Iran
summary: Iran negotiates a possible toll in the strait as Trump denies the blockade and an attack on a merchant raises tension.

title: Israel holds general elections on October 27 with Prime Minister Netanyahu in the spotlight
summary: The early elections will decide whether Netanyahu remains at the head of the government amid criticism and protests.

title: A fire in a Bangkok restaurant kills at least 27 people
summary: Most of the victims were trapped in the bathrooms as they could not escape from the flames in the night venue.

title: Jannik Sinner defeats Alexander Zverev and wins his second consecutive Wimbledon
summary: The Italian adds his fifth Grand Slam title by defeating the German tennis player in the final of the London tournament.

title: China intensifies its claims in the South China Sea and criticizes Japan
summary: On the tenth anniversary of the arbitration ruling, Beijing claims jurisdiction in the Japanese EEZ and attacks Foreign Minister Motegi.

title: Japan expresses concern over rising long-term bond yields
summary: The government seeks to dispel expectations of a rate hike and reaffirms the independence of the Bank of Japan.

title: France, Spain, England and Argentina will compete in the World Cup semifinals
summary: The tournament resumes with the matchups set after the elimination of Switzerland and Norway, and with big stars on the field.

title: Japanese Diet faces final stretch with legislative gridlocks and criticism of the government
summary: With only five days remaining, the opposition demands progress and the ruling party tries to avoid extending the session.

title: Food prices in Japan show divergent trends between rice and imports
summary: The depreciation of the yen drives up import prices while rice becomes cheaper after the shortage, affecting purchasing power.

title: Floods in Bangladesh leave more than 50 dead and over 600 rescued
summary: Torrential rains cause landslides and a serious humanitarian crisis in the South Asian country.

title: Japan dominates wheelchair Wimbledon: Oda wins and Ueda achieves double
summary: Tokito Oda wins his second consecutive Wimbledon and Yui Ueda is crowned in singles and women's doubles.

title: NYT points to Japan as a technology supply base for the Russian army
summary: Russian intelligence would use Japan to acquire high-tech components for military purposes in Ukraine.

title: Former Emir of Qatar Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, founder of Al Jazeera, dies
summary: The monarch, who drove the country's modernization and the organization of the 2022 World Cup, died at age 74.

title: Young woman disappears after a jet ski capsizes off the coast of Chiba
summary: Search teams continue efforts to locate the woman, in her 20s, who fell into the sea.

title: Japan reports multiple bear incidents, including home invasions and an emergency hunt
summary: Bear activity in inhabited areas intensifies, leading authorities to issue alerts and apply control protocols.

title: TSMC transforms Kumamoto into a semiconductor hub attracting Taiwan
summary: The presence of the Taiwanese chip giant is generating an economic and technological boom in the Japanese prefecture.
